codeReview.summary: The feature adds substantial cross-surface harness coverage, but it does not fully implement the required mock/live equivalence proof and its mocked Realtime bootstrap coverage stops before the websocket hop.

issues.description: VAL-CROSS-004 requires a paired mock/live contract harness with normalized assertions, but this test only emits a live `CrossSurfaceReport` and cleanup line. The companion mock harness in `tests/cross_surface_contract.rs` performs standalone assertions at lines 40/114/303/403 and never produces the same normalized report shape or compares mock vs live outcomes, so the feature does not actually verify mock/live equivalence for request metadata, status class, or terminal-state interpretation.

The mocked REST-to-Realtime bootstrap test stops at `prepare_ws_target` plus header/url assertions. It never opens a mocked websocket session or exercises a typed failure/success path across the websocket hop, even though VAL-CROSS-009 calls for a mock REST-and-WebSocket integration test. As written, the mocked half of the REST→Realtime bootstrap contract remains uncovered.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `sdk-surface-worker` skill does not explicitly say that proof-only cross-surface work may legitimately need a tiny production helper when a stronger contract harness exposes a consumer-ergonomics gap, even if no transport/runtime defect is found.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: /Users/staticpayload/Mainframe/openai-rust/.factory/skills/sdk-surface-worker/SKILL.md:17,25 frame proof-only work as assertion-first and runtime changes only when stronger proof exposes a real defect; /Users/staticpayload/.factory/missions/beaa247f-5975-4da4-acc2-19deef388841/handoffs/2026-04-06T10-01-43-842Z__cross-surface-integration-harnesses__72e89088-4dbd-4e2c-8530-1c40ba010755.json:129-132 records the same gap in `suggestedChanges` for small helpers like webhook routing accessors.

summary: Fail: the new harnesses cover many cross-surface behaviors, but they do not satisfy the required paired mock/live equivalence proof for VAL-CROSS-004, and the mocked REST-to-Realtime coverage does not traverse the websocket hop required by VAL-CROSS-009.
